strategy_outcomes.name: SOL Balance Reclaim Continuation v1
strategy_outcomes.edge_mechanism: deeper pullback into structural support followed by value reclaim and modest continuation
strategy_outcomes.hypothesis: SOL 4h is the best-fit primary lane for testing whether repaired pullbacks inside a broad range can still deliver a modest continuation leg after value is reclaimed. The edge is not full trend capture; it is a short continuation burst after balance repair.
strategy_outcomes.rationale: 0 trades on valid data (integrity_skip:zero_trades_both_samples; integrity_skip:zero_trades_both_samples; integrity_skip:zero_trades_both_samples) — Quandalf must explicitly choose iterate or abort.

strategy_outcomes.name: ETH Squeeze Hold Release v1
strategy_outcomes.edge_mechanism: volatility squeeze release that only enters after post-break persistence above fast value
strategy_outcomes.hypothesis: ETH 4h is the cleaner similar-asset lane for a squeeze-release test because range compression on ETH can resolve into a brief persistence phase even when the broader regime is choppy. The edge should come from entering after the breakout proves it can hold, not from paying up on the first bar.

long: Exit remaining 20% on close back through EMA_55 or 4 bars
short: Exit remaining 20% on close back through EMA_55 or 4 bars

strategy_outcomes.name: TAO Failed Break Repair v1
strategy_outcomes.edge_mechanism: failed breakdown reclaim and repair into balance rather than strict hold choreography
strategy_outcomes.hypothesis: TAO 4h is the structurally different lane for testing failed-break repair, but expressed as a broader reclaim-and-repair structure rather than the already-killed strict failed-breakdown-hold grammar. In a mixed chop backdrop, downside excursions should fail often enough to produce tradable repairs back into value.

long: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.12 * ATR_14 by selling 15%
short: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.12 * ATR_14 by covering 15%

strategy_outcomes.rationale: abort: fail with QS 1.14, PF 1.24, DD 10.2%, trades 5

strategy_outcomes.name: TAO Range Failure Reacceptance v1
strategy_outcomes.edge_mechanism: multi-bar breakout loses acceptance and re-enters balance for a repair move
strategy_outcomes.hypothesis: TAO 4h is the best-fit primary lane for a range-failure reacceptance structure in a mixed chop/trend-up backdrop because it tends to overextend beyond balance and then snap back once acceptance fails. The edge is balance repair after failed expansion, not breakout chasing.
strategy_outcomes.rationale: abort: fail with QS 1.83, PF 1.88, DD 5.2%, trades 5

strategy_outcomes.name: ETH Trend Reset Continuation v1
strategy_outcomes.edge_mechanism: deeper pullback reset into value followed by reclaim and modest continuation
strategy_outcomes.hypothesis: ETH 4h is the cleaner similar-asset lane for testing whether a deeper reset continuation can still work inside a mixed chop/trend-up structure without relying on shallow EMA touches. The edge should come from repaired pullbacks that regain directional control and travel enough for a modest second leg.

long: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.12 * ATR_14 by selling 15%
short: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.12 * ATR_14 by covering 15%

strategy_outcomes.name: AXS Compression Fakeout Reexpansion v1
strategy_outcomes.edge_mechanism: compression fakeout loses acceptance, reclaims direction, then re-expands with confirmation
strategy_outcomes.hypothesis: AXS 4h is the structurally different lane for testing whether compression fakeouts can reverse and re-expand more profitably than plain first-break entries. In a mixed chop 4h environment, false initial breaks should occur often enough for a reclaim-and-corrected-expansion setup to matter.
strategy_outcomes.rationale: 0 trades on valid data (integrity_skip:zero_trades_both_samples; integrity_skip:zero_trades_both_samples) — Quandalf must explicitly choose iterate or abort.

guidance.instruction: Work strategy-by-strategy and queue-row-by-queue-row. For each spec, choose exactly one overall decision: pass, refine, or abort. Inside that strategy decision, include one queue_decision for every queue row in the packet. If a strategy produced 0 trades on valid data, treat that as a red flag that must resolve explicitly as either refine or abort. Do not omit any tested row and do not use promote, fix_only, skip, pending, or undecided.
